---
title: "Helpful links"
---

- My notes for the vectors stuff of MA12001 are here: <https://dundeemath.github.io/MA12001-algebra/_site/>. This module builds directly on this (particularly chapters 1 and 3) so it may be helpful to revise this.

- Most of the important results in this module are here: <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vector_calculus_identities>. Your formula sheet in the exam should be based off this.

- The curvilinear co-ordinate results are summarised here: <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Del_in_cylindrical_and_spherical_coordinates>.

- On YouTube, you can find many good videos on all parts of this module. I particularly like 3Blue1Brown:
    - Div and curl: <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rB83DpBJQsE>
    - Fourier series: <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=r6sGWTCMz2k>
    - Fourier transforms: <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=spUNpyF58BY>
    - Convolutions: <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IaSGqQa5O-M>
